Experimental Shared-Memory Backend for Parallel MVPA

Source: R/shard_backend.R
shard_backend.Rd

Uses the shard package to place the full data matrix in POSIX shared memory, enabling zero-copy parallel ROI extraction across furrr workers. Activated per model spec via use_shard.

Details

When enabled, the default serial ROI-extraction loop in mvpa_iterate is skipped. Instead, each worker receives lightweight ALTREP handles that point into the shared-memory segment and extracts its own ROI columns on demand. This eliminates the two main memory bottlenecks of the default pipeline:

  1. Serial extract_roi() in the main process.

  2. Serialisation of large ROI matrices to workers via furrr.

Supported dataset types

  • mvpa_image_dataset (volumetric)

  • mvpa_surface_dataset (cortical surface)

  • mvpa_clustered_dataset (parcellated)

Cleanup

Shared-memory segments are released automatically at the end of mvpa_iterate(). You can also call shard_cleanup(mod_spec$shard_data) explicitly.

On this page
